Claim: Moral-emotional language increases diffusion of political/moral content; engagement favors outrage signals.
Notes: Supports misalignment between governance (friction) and viral dynamics.

Claim: False news spreads faster/farther than true news; human novelty-seeking drives sharing.
Notes: Supports preference for immediacy/novelty over accuracy.

Claim: Trust & Safety / moderation is labor-intensive and economically/organizationally precarious; governance adds ongoing cost.
Notes: Supports cost and fragility of governance operations.

Claim: Content moderation is a large and growing services market, implying substantial cost/need at scale.
Notes: Suggests governance is expensive; ad model tension.

Claim: Consumers show mixed willingness to pay for social media; subscription willingness is limited/price-sensitive.
Notes: Supports niche viability and pricing constraints.

Claim: Regulatory mechanisms for platform observability/transparency are emerging, increasing governance expectations.
Notes: Supports institutional/regulatory alignment even if user adoption lags.
